experiments.name: test_02
experiments.idea: using the LMOP Landfill and Project Database, and census data can we find anything interesting about the demographics of the locations landfills are placed?

experiments.name: test_03
experiments.idea: https://oui.doleta.gov/unemploy/index.asp this is data about unemployment inssurance. download it, look through it, and come up with something interesting an novel to write about MAKE SURE IT IS INTERESTING if possible

experiments.name: test_04
experiments.idea: https://deportationdata.org/--> look through all of the datasets avliable from this site along with any others that might prove useful to generate a paper on an interesting an novel finding

experiments.name: test_05
experiments.idea: look at private school data and compare it with politiucal and demographic data to see if polotics can lead to interestung insights into the education system at the local and state level. here are some links to get started with https://nces.ed.gov/, https://nces.ed.gov/surveys/pss/index.asp, https://nces.ed.gov/surveys/pss/pssdata.asp, https://projects.propublica.org/private-school-demographics, https://www.propublica.org/events/how-to-use-our-private-school-demographics-news-app, https://www.propublica.org/series/segregation-academies

experiments.name: test_06
experiments.idea: How do different locations react and recover from extreme weather events: do demographics, polotics, or policys imporve or hurt economic recovery?
